title: Wiki Workflow Hardening Implementation Plan
description: subagent 검증에서 발견된 skill/CLI/MCP 품질 경계 문제를 테스트 우선으로 개선하는 Stenc 실행 계획.

page.humanSummary: 이 계획은 문서 계약 정정, ingest/draft 안전장치, query scaffolding, workflow CLI/MCP를 순서대로 구현한다.
page.agentSummary: Implement wiki workflow hardening in test-first slices: align draft and skill docs around publish-draft, add no-clobber source ingest, enforce section-level human-lock preservation, standardize query reports/capture scaffolds, add workflow CLI checkpoint commands, expose MCP parity, and run full wiki/Stenc verification.

body.goal: LLM Wiki agent skills and CLI/MCP workflows를 실제 운영에서 안전하게 사용할 수 있도록 subagent findings를 코드, 문서, 테스트에 반영한다.
body.architecture: Repo-local skills remain the cognition layer. tools/wiki library functions provide deterministic checks and reports. CLI and MCP expose the same deterministic checkpoint behavior while durable semantic knowledge still enters through publish-draft.

workerInstructions.note: 각 slice는 fail-first 테스트 또는 문서 검증 기준을 먼저 추가하고, 해당 slice만 통과시킨 뒤 다음 slice로 이동한다.

scopeCheck.assessment: 이 계획은 하나의 품질 hardening 프로젝트다. docs, deterministic guard, CLI/MCP workflow surface가 함께 움직여야 하므로 분리된 프로젝트로 나누면 계약 drift가 생긴다.
scopeCheck.decomposition: 계약 정정, ingest no-clobber, section lock 보존, query scaffold, workflow CLI, MCP parity, 문서/검증 순서로 나눈다.

body.currentState: 완료 전 상태는 wiki-ingest/wiki-update/wiki-query skill docs, publish-draft, health, MCP wiki_health/wiki_publish_draft만 갖춘 상태였다. docs/agent/DRAFTS.md는 apply-draft 중심 durable flow를 안내했고, ingest-source는 slug 기반 source page를 바로 썼으며, apply-draft는 page-level human lock만 차단했다.
body.targetState: 현재 목표 상태는 agent가 세 skill을 별도 추측 없이 실행할 수 있고, source ingest가 기존 source page를 clobber하지 않으며, draft publish가 section-level human lock을 보존하는 것이다. Query reports and capture drafts have stable scaffolds. workflow CLI/MCP commands provide deterministic checkpoint behavior and explicitly leave semantic extraction/query answering to the agent.

in: Stenc validation and rendered page generation
out: Automatic semantic page extraction inside CLI
Git commit unless explicitly requested after implementation

fileStructure.responsibility: Add documentation contract checks for publish-draft, skill completion gates, and absence of misleading workflow wording.
Make publish-draft the default durable path and demote apply-draft to low-level dry-run/debug use.
Clarify duplicate handling, source_count/provenance requirements, contradiction handling, and final validation.
Clarify section-lock behavior, report versus review routing, draft template, index/log expectations, and final validation.
Define query report filename/sections, mandatory citations for substantive answers, and answer-and-capture draft flow.
Add fail-first tests for no-clobber ingest, section lock preservation, workflow CLI commands, query report scaffold, and MCP parity.
Implement idempotent source registration, drift/collision failure, human-lock protection, and richer ingest reports.
Reject drafts that alter existing human-locked sections while preserving page-level lock behavior.
Provide shared deterministic workflow preflight, publish, and report-scaffold helpers.
Add workflow ingest/update/query subcommands backed by tools/wiki/lib/workflow.py.
Expose workflow checkpoint tools with CLI-equivalent behavior.
Show simplified human workflow while naming workflow CLI/MCP as checkpoints, not semantic generators.
Explain internal processing with no-clobber ingest, section lock preservation, query report/capture, and workflow checkpoints.
Update final architecture and next-step status for workflow hardening.
Keep the short agent contract aligned with the hardened flow.

slices.title: Contract drift and skill hardening

files.role: Add tests that reject apply-draft-centered durable flow and require skill-specific hardening terms.
Replace durable apply instructions with publish-draft instructions.
Add duplicate, provenance, contradiction, and final validation rules.
Add section lock, report/review routing, draft template, index/log, and final validation rules.
Add report filename, report sections, mandatory citations, capture draft, and final validation rules.

steps.title: Write failing documentation contract tests
steps.instruction: In tests/test_wiki_quality_baseline.py, add tests that docs/agent/DRAFTS.md contains publish-draft as the required durable publication command, does not list apply-draft --report as the normal durable flow, and that the three skill docs mention their new hardening responsibilities.

steps.title: Update DRAFTS.md
steps.instruction: Rewrite the Required Flow so it uses publish-draft --report for durable publication. Keep apply-draft --dry-run as an optional low-level validation command only.

steps.title: Harden wiki-ingest skill
steps.instruction: Add explicit source duplicate criteria: same canonical_source plus same hash is unchanged, same canonical_source plus different hash is drift, same slug plus different source is collision. Require source_count/provenance updates for substantive pages and final lint --report or health success.

steps.title: Harden wiki-update skill
steps.instruction: Add exact section-lock preservation expectation, scratch/reports for proposed patches to locked content, scratch/review for contested decisions or important contradictions, and index/log requirements for durable changes.

steps.title: Harden wiki-query skill
steps.instruction: Add scratch/reports/YYYY-MM-DD-query-<slug>.md naming, required report sections, mandatory durable citations for substantive synthesis, and answer-and-capture draft template plus publish-draft path.

steps.title: Verify documentation contract
steps.instruction: Run the baseline tests after docs are updated.

doneWhen: DRAFTS.md no longer instructs apply-draft --report as the normal durable path.
Each skill contains enough operational detail for an agent to execute without guessing.
Baseline documentation tests pass.

slices.title: Source ingest no-clobber

files.role: Add tests for idempotent same source, source drift, slug collision, and locked source page behavior.
Implement no-clobber and richer report outcomes.
Print clear ingest outcomes and preserve nonzero exits for drift/collision/lock.

steps.title: Write failing idempotency test
steps.instruction: Create a temp vault, ingest the same raw source twice, and assert the second run exits successfully without duplicating index/log entries and reports outcome unchanged.

steps.title: Write failing drift and collision tests
steps.instruction: Test same canonical source with changed hash and same slug with different canonical source. Both should fail without overwriting the existing source page.

steps.title: Write failing locked source page test
steps.instruction: Test that ingest-source fails when the existing target source page contains <!-- human-locked -->.

steps.title: Implement ingest outcome model
steps.instruction: Extend IngestResult with outcome values such as created and unchanged. Raise ValueError with clear messages for source-drift, slug-collision, and human-locked target. Read existing target frontmatter before writing.

steps.title: Make index/log idempotent for unchanged ingest
steps.instruction: When outcome is unchanged, do not call apply_index_entry or append_log_entry. Still allow report output that explains no durable change was made.

steps.title: Verify ingest safety
steps.instruction: Run focused tests after implementation.

doneWhen: Repeated same-source ingest is idempotent.
Drift, collision, and locked source target do not overwrite existing source pages.
Reports expose created/unchanged/drift/collision outcomes.

slices.title: Section-level human-lock preservation

files.role: Add tests for changed versus preserved human-locked sections in dry-run and publish paths.
Compare existing locked ranges with draft-rendered locked ranges and reject modified locked content.

steps.title: Write failing locked-section mutation test
steps.instruction: Create an existing page with a human-locked:start/end section. Prepare a draft that changes text inside the locked range. Assert apply-draft --dry-run and publish-draft both fail before writing.
steps.expected: FAIL before exact range preservation exists.

steps.title: Write preserved locked-section positive test
steps.instruction: Prepare a draft that changes content outside the locked section while preserving the locked range exactly. Assert dry-run succeeds.
steps.expected: FAIL until preservation comparison handles valid changes.

steps.title: Implement locked range extraction comparison
steps.instruction: Use the existing human lock parsing behavior as the source of marker truth. Compare the full text between each start/end marker in the current page and rendered draft. Reject missing, reordered, or changed locked ranges.

steps.title: Verify publish rollback is not the first defense
steps.instruction: Ensure the locked-section failure happens during dry-run validation before durable write, not after post-lint rollback.
steps.expected: PASS and failure message mentions human-locked section preservation.

doneWhen: Drafts cannot alter section-level locked content.
Drafts can alter unlocked content around preserved locked sections.
Failure happens before durable mutation.

slices.title: Query report and capture scaffolds

files.role: Start shared workflow helper module with query report scaffold creation.
Add workflow query --question --prepare-report and workflow query --draft --publish --report.
Add CLI tests for query report scaffold and query capture publish routing.

steps.title: Write failing query report scaffold test
steps.instruction: Assert workflow query --question '...' --prepare-report creates scratch/reports/YYYY-MM-DD-query-<slug>.md with required sections: Question, Answer Summary, Pages Consulted, Sources Consulted, Confidence, Contradictions, Reusable Capture Recommendation.

steps.title: Implement query report scaffold helper
steps.instruction: Create tools/wiki/lib/workflow.py with deterministic slugging, report path resolution, and query report scaffold rendering. The scaffold must state that semantic answer synthesis is still required from the agent.

steps.title: Add query publish wrapper
steps.instruction: Add workflow query --draft <path> --publish --report as a thin publish-draft wrapper for answer-and-capture drafts.

steps.title: Verify query workflow commands
steps.instruction: Run focused workflow query tests.
steps.expected: PASS for query workflow command cases.

doneWhen: answer-with-report has a stable report scaffold.
answer-and-capture uses publish-draft through the workflow command.
Workflow output clearly says semantic synthesis is agent work.

slices.title: Workflow ingest and update CLI checkpoints

files.role: Add ingest and update checkpoint helpers.
Add workflow ingest and workflow update subcommands.
Add tests for workflow ingest, update preflight, and update publish.

steps.title: Write failing workflow ingest test
steps.instruction: Assert workflow ingest --source raw/sources/example.md --report validates raw location, delegates source registration behavior, writes or references an ingest report, and prints semantic extraction pending.

steps.title: Write failing workflow update preflight test
steps.instruction: Assert workflow update --target wiki/concepts/example.md --preflight --report reads target existence, reports page-level and section-level lock state, and writes a preflight report without mutating the page.
steps.expected: FAIL before workflow update preflight exists.

steps.title: Write failing workflow update publish test
steps.instruction: Assert workflow update --target wiki/concepts/example.md --draft scratch/drafts/example.json --publish --report routes through publish-draft and fails if draft target differs from --target.
steps.expected: FAIL before workflow update publish exists.

steps.title: Implement workflow ingest helper and CLI
steps.instruction: Add prepare_ingest_workflow and CLI routing. Keep output deterministic and avoid semantic extraction.

steps.title: Implement workflow update helpers and CLI
steps.instruction: Add prepare_update_workflow and publish_update_workflow. Use existing draft loading/target validation where possible to avoid duplicate semantics.

steps.title: Verify workflow CLI checkpoints
steps.instruction: Run focused tests for workflow ingest/update/query.

doneWhen: workflow ingest/update/query commands exist.
Commands never claim to perform semantic extraction or answer generation.
Publish variants use publish-draft behavior.

slices.title: MCP workflow parity

files.role: Add MCP tool list and call tests for workflow checkpoint tools.
Expose workflow checkpoint tools backed by CLI/lib behavior.

steps.title: Write failing MCP tool list test
steps.instruction: Assert tools/list includes wiki_workflow_ingest, wiki_workflow_update_preflight, wiki_workflow_update_publish, wiki_workflow_query_prepare, and wiki_workflow_query_publish.

steps.title: Write failing MCP call smoke tests
steps.instruction: Add at least one non-mutating call test for query_prepare or update_preflight and one publish wrapper argument construction test.

steps.title: Implement MCP tool schemas and dispatch
steps.instruction: Add tool schemas and map each tool to the matching workflow CLI arguments. Keep business logic in CLI/lib helpers.

steps.title: Verify MCP parity
steps.instruction: Run focused tests after MCP dispatch is added.
steps.expected: PASS for MCP workflow parity cases.

doneWhen: MCP exposes all workflow checkpoint tools.
MCP and CLI return equivalent deterministic output.
MCP does not duplicate semantic logic.

slices.title: Human docs and final verification

files.role: Update user-facing examples for hardened workflow behavior.
Update internal processing explanation for no-clobber, section locks, query scaffolds, and workflow checkpoints.
Record workflow hardening as the next architecture stage.
Keep short operational instructions aligned with the hardened flow.

steps.title: Update human docs
steps.instruction: Explain workflow CLI/MCP as checkpoints, not semantic generators. Keep usage.md simple and architecture.md technical.

steps.title: Update LLM-WIKI and AGENTS
steps.instruction: Add hardened command names and completion gate updates without expanding AGENTS.md beyond its concise contract.

steps.title: Run full unit tests
steps.instruction: Run the required unit test suite.

steps.title: Run wiki lint
steps.instruction: Run deterministic whole-vault lint with report output.

steps.title: Run wiki health
steps.instruction: Run the whole-vault health facade.

steps.title: Validate and render Stenc docs
steps.instruction: Validate both Stenc docs, regenerate pages, and verify rendered pages exist.
steps.command: node tools/stenc/validate-stenc-doc.js docs/stenc/content/specs/2026-05-30-wiki-workflow-hardening.spec.json && node tools/stenc/validate-stenc-doc.js docs/stenc/content/plans/2026-05-30-wiki-workflow-hardening-implementation.plan.json && node tools/stenc/setup-project.js --project-root "$(pwd)" --docs-dir docs/stenc && node tools/stenc/check-rendered-pages.js docs/stenc
steps.expected: Both JSON files are valid and rendered pages are present.

steps.title: Check working tree
steps.instruction: Inspect git status and summarize modified files before asking whether to commit.

doneWhen: Human and agent docs agree on the hardened workflow.
Full unit tests, lint, health, and Stenc validation pass.
Final report separates implemented behavior from future semantic automation.

risks.risk: workflow command names may imply semantic extraction or answer generation.
risks.mitigation: Every workflow output and doc section must state that semantic extraction/query synthesis is agent work and CLI/MCP only perform deterministic checkpoints.

risks.risk: section lock comparison may be too brittle if whitespace changes inside a locked section are harmless.
risks.mitigation: Use exact preservation intentionally. human-locked means human-owned; harmless edits still require a proposed patch or review item.

risks.risk: source ingest no-clobber can block legitimate source refreshes.
risks.mitigation: Treat changed hash as drift and require an explicit future drift-resolution workflow instead of silent overwrite.

risks.risk: MCP wrappers can drift from CLI semantics.
risks.mitigation: Implement MCP as argument routing to the same CLI/lib behavior and cover tool list plus call behavior in tests.

validation.purpose: Verify baseline, CLI, MCP, ingest, draft, and workflow behavior.
Verify whole-vault deterministic quality with report output.
Confirm rendered pages exist for all Stenc JSON docs.

agentInstructions: Use this plan task-by-task; do not jump straight to convenience wrappers.
Write failing tests before implementation for each code slice.
Preserve raw/ immutability and human locks.
Keep CLI/MCP workflow commands deterministic and explicit about semantic work remaining in the agent layer.
After each slice, run the narrow test command for that slice before moving on.

selfReviewChecks.name: Spec coverage
selfReviewChecks.purpose: Confirm every requirement in the paired spec maps to a slice and concrete test or doc update.

selfReviewChecks.name: Quality-first behavior
selfReviewChecks.purpose: Confirm implemented changes improve durable wiki quality instead of merely shortening commands.

selfReviewChecks.name: No semantic overclaim
selfReviewChecks.purpose: Confirm workflow CLI/MCP outputs do not claim to create knowledge without an agent-authored draft.

options.label: Subagent-Driven
options.description: Dispatch a fresh subagent per independent slice and review between slices.

options.label: Inline Execution
options.description: Execute slices in this session with checkpointed verification after each slice.

supportingSections.heading: No Placeholders
supportingSections.content: This plan intentionally names exact files, commands, expected outputs, and workflow command contracts so the next implementation step can proceed without redesign.
items: Do not replace this plan with broad TODOs.
Do not move semantic extraction into workflow.py.
Do not mark implementation complete without unit tests, lint, health, and Stenc checks.

facts.label: durable facade
facts.value: publish-draft already exists and should remain the durable mutation facade.

facts.label: workflow scope
facts.value: workflow commands are additive checkpoints over existing quality gates.

facts.label: source drift
facts.value: source drift resolution is intentionally deferred to a future explicit workflow.

links.label: Spec
links.purpose: Canonical behavior contract implemented by this plan.

links.label: CLI
links.purpose: Command surface that will expose workflow checkpoint commands.

links.label: MCP server
links.purpose: Agent tool surface that will mirror workflow checkpoint commands.
